content:
# Oregon 2-Night Trip Itinerary

## Day 1: Portland Exploration

### Morning (9am-12pm)
- **Powell's City of Books** – World's largest independent bookstore
- Walk the Pearl District galleries and boutiques
- Coffee at Stumptown Coffee Roasters
- **Cost:** Free (books optional $20-50) | **Transport:** Walking

### Afternoon (12pm-5pm)
- **Lunch:** Portland Food Cart Pods – try various cuisines ($10-15)
- **OMSI** – Interactive science museum + submarine tour (2-3 hours)
- **Cost:** $37 ($22 admission + $15 lunch) | **Transport:** TriMet MAX ($2.50)

### Evening (5pm-9pm)
- Walk along the Willamette River waterfront
- **Dinner:** Alberta Arts District restaurants ($25-40)
- **Dessert:** Voodoo Doughnut ($5)
- **Cost:** $35-45 | **Transport:** Uber ($15)

**Day 1 Total: ~$80-130** (excluding accommodation)

---

## Day 2: Columbia River Gorge Day Trip

### Morning (8am-12pm)
- Grab coffee and breakfast to-go ($10)
- Drive to **Multnomah Falls** (30 min) – hike to Benson Bridge viewpoint
- Scenic drive on Historic Columbia River Highway
- **Cost:** $15 ($5 parking + $10 breakfast) | **Transport:** Rental car ($65/day)

### Afternoon (12pm-5pm)
- Hike **Wahclella Falls** or **Latourell Falls** (1-2 hours)
- **Lunch:** Thunder Island Brewing in Cascade Locks ($15-20)
- Drive to **Vista House** at Crown Point for panoramic views
- **Cost:** $20 | **Transport:** Rental car (included)

### Evening (5pm-9pm)
- Return to Portland (1 hour drive)
- **Dinner:** Craft brewery in Pearl District ($30-40)
- Evening stroll, optional dessert
- **Cost:** $35-45 | **Transport:** Walking

**Day 2 Total: ~$135-145** (including car rental, excluding accommodation)

---

## Day 3: Mt. Hood & Departure

### Morning (8am-12pm)
- Early breakfast at hotel or nearby café ($15)
- Drive to **Timberline Lodge** (1.5 hours) – explore the historic lodge
- Short alpine hike or enjoy mountain views
- **Cost:** $15 | **Transport:** Rental car (half-day ~$35)

### Afternoon (12pm-3pm)
- **Lunch:** Timberline Lodge dining room ($20-30)
- Drive back to Portland, return rental car, head to airport
- **Cost:** $60 ($25 lunch + $35 car) | **Transport:** Rental car → airport shuttle

**Day 3 Total: ~$75-90**

---

## Quick Reference

### Accommodation
| Level | Area | Price/Night |
|-------|------|-------------|
| Budget | Downtown/Old Town | $80-120 |
| Mid-range | Pearl District/Alberta Arts | $150-220 |
| Luxury | Nob Hill/Waterfront | $300+ |

### Transport
| Mode | Cost |
|------|------|
| TriMet day pass | $5 |
| Uber/Lyft | $15-25/ride |
| Car Rental | $50-80/day |

### Local Tips
- No sales tax in Oregon
- No self-serve gas (attendants pump)
- Tip 18-22% at restaurants
- Bring cash for some food carts
- Bring layers – gorge can be windy

---

## Budget Summary

| Category | Budget | Mid-Range | Luxury |
|----------|--------|-----------|--------|
| Accommodation (2 nights) | $160-240 | $300-440 | $600+ |
| Food & Drinks | $90-120 | $140-180 | $250+ |
| Transport | $100-130 | $120-150 | $150+ |
| Activities | $30-50 | $50-80 | $100+ |
| **TOTAL** | **$380-540** | **$610-850** | **$1,100+** |
rawContent:
# Oregon 2-Night Trip Itinerary

---

## ATTRACTIONS

### 🌲 Nature
| Attraction | Cost |
|------------|------|
| Crater Lake National Park | $30/vehicle |
| Columbia River Gorge & Multnomah Falls | Free ($5 parking) |
| Oregon Coast (Cannon Beach) | Free |
| Smith Rock State Park | $5 day-use |

### 🎭 Cultural
| Attraction | Cost |
|------------|------|
| Powell's Books (Pearl District) | Free (books $20-50) |
| Oregon Shakespeare Festival | $35-120/ticket |
| OMSI Museum | $17-22/adult |

### 🍴 Food & Drink
| Attraction | Cost |
|------------|------|
| Willamette Valley Wine Country | $15-25/tasting |
| Portland Food Carts & Voodoo Doughnut | $5-15/meal |

### 🎪 Entertainment
| Attraction | Cost |
|------------|------|
| Mt. Hood & Timberline Lodge | Free visit (lifts $80-130) |

---

## LOGISTICS

### Accommodation Areas
- **Budget:** Downtown/Old Town – $80-120/night
- **Mid-range:** Pearl District or Alberta Arts – $150-220/night
- **Luxury:** Nob Hill/Waterfront – $300+/night

### Transport Options
| Mode | Cost |
|------|------|
| TriMet (bus/MAX/streetcar) | $2.50/ride, $5 day pass |
| Uber/Lyft | $15-25/ride |
| Car Rental | $50-80/day (essential for day trips) |

### Daily Budget (Mid-Range)
- Accommodation: $150-220
- Food: $60-90
- Transport: $30-50
- Activities: $50-80
- **Daily Total: $290-440**

### Local Tips
- No sales tax in Oregon
- No self-serve gas (attendants pump for you)
- Tip 18-22% at restaurants
- Some food carts are cash-only
- Cards accepted almost everywhere

---

# 📅 Day-by-Day Itinerary

## Day 1: Portland Exploration

### Morning (9am-12pm)
- **Powell's City of Books** – World's largest independent bookstore
- Walk the Pearl District galleries and boutiques
- ☕ Coffee at Stumptown Coffee Roasters
- **Cost:** Free (books optional $20-50)
- **Transport:** Walking (Pearl District is very walkable)

### Afternoon (12pm-5pm)
- 🍴 **Lunch:** Portland Food Cart Pods – try various cuisines ($10-15)
- **OMSI** – Interactive science museum + submarine tour (2-3 hours)
- **Cost:** $22 admission + $15 lunch = $37
- **Transport:** TriMet MAX to OMSI ($2.50)

### Evening (5pm-9pm)
- Walk along the Willamette River waterfront
- 🍴 **Dinner:** Alberta Arts District restaurants ($25-40)
- 🍩 **Dessert:** Voodoo Doughnut ($5)
- **Cost:** $35-45
- **Transport:** Uber to Alberta ($15)

**Day 1 Total: ~$80-130** (excluding accommodation)

---

## Day 2: Columbia River Gorge Day Trip

### Morning (8am-12pm)
- ☕ Grab coffee and breakfast to-go in Portland ($10)
- Drive to **Multnomah Falls** (30 min) – hike to Benson Bridge viewpoint
- Continue scenic drive on Historic Columbia River Highway
- **Cost:** $5 parking + $10 breakfast = $15
- **Transport:** Rental car ($65/day)

### Afternoon (12pm-5pm)
- Hike **Wahclella Falls** or **Latourell Falls** (choose one, 1-2 hours)
- 🍴 **Lunch:** Thunder Island Brewing in Cascade Locks ($15-20)
- Drive to **Vista House** at Crown Point for panoramic views
- **Cost:** Free hiking + $20 lunch = $20
- **Transport:** Rental car (included)

### Evening (5pm-9pm)
- Return to Portland (1 hour drive)
- 🍴 **Dinner:** Craft brewery in Pearl District ($30-40)
- Evening stroll, optional dessert
- **Cost:** $35-45
- **Transport:** Walking from hotel

**Day 2 Total: ~$135-145** (including car rental, excluding accommodation)

---

## Day 3: Mt. Hood & Departure

### Morning (8am-12pm)
- Early breakfast at hotel or nearby café ($15)
- Drive to **Timberline Lodge** (1.5 hours) – explore the historic lodge
- Short alpine hike or enjoy mountain views
- **Cost:** Free admission + $15 breakfast = $15
- **Transport:** Rental car (half-day ~$35)

### Afternoon (12pm-3pm)
- 🍴 **Lunch:** Timberline Lodge dining room ($20-30)
- Drive back to Portland
- Return rental car, head to airport
- **Cost:** $25 lunch + $35 car = $60
- **Transport:** Rental car → airport shuttle

**Day 3 Total: ~$75-90**

---

# 💰 Total Trip Budget Estimate

| Category | Budget | Mid-Range | Luxury |
|----------|--------|-----------|--------|
| Accommodation (2 nights) | $160-240 | $300-440 | $600+ |
| Food & Drinks | $90-120 | $140-180 | $250+ |
| Transport (car + local) | $100-130 | $120-150 | $150+ |
| Activities & Entry Fees | $30-50 | $50-80 | $100+ |
| **TOTAL** | **$380-540** | **$610-850** | **$1,100+** |

---

### 💡 Pro Tips
- Book Pearl District hotel for best walkability
- Reserve rental car in advance for Day 2-3
- Bring layers – gorge can be windy even in summer
- No sales tax = save on any shopping!
- Cash recommended for some food carts
